title: activity — health probes
description: Health probes registered with Foundation's health aggregator (surfaced via `GET /api/health`). Activity is a user-visible surface — a broken feed is a customer-facing bug. Probes have moderate tolerance for latency because activity capture must never block domain writes.

probes.name: activity-log-table-reachable
probes.description: Verifies the `activity_log` table is reachable + accepts writes (dry-run insert wrapped in a rolled-back transaction). Critical — activity capture is a soft dependency for domain writes, but a broken table breaks every LogsActivity trait fire.

probes.name: activity-log-name-catalog-loaded
probes.description: Verifies data/log-names-catalog.json loaded successfully at boot. LogsActivity trait's boot-time validation depends on it — a missing catalog means every trait fire refuses at model boot.

probes.name: activity-vendor-migration-applied
probes.description: Verifies the vendor's `create_activity_log_table` migration ran AND our `add_tenant_id_and_application_id_to_activity_log_table` overlay ran. Missing overlay = activity writes fail (tenant_id NOT NULL constraint violation). Fails startup — halts boot until the migration is applied.

probes.name: activity-backfill-complete
probes.description: Counts rows WHERE tenant_id IS NULL AND properties->>'tenant_id' IS NOT NULL. Should be zero post-Wave-6 upgrade. Non-critical — the backfill job may still be running; the count decreases monotonically. Alerts fire when the count has been non-zero for >48h.

probes.name: activity-broadcast-reachable
probes.description: Verifies the reverb broadcast layer is reachable + accepts a synthetic broadcast to the health-check channel. Non-critical because the frontend falls back to polling when broadcast is unavailable — slower but correct.

probes.name: activity-jobs-not-stalled
probes.description: Reads depth + oldest-job age for the `activity` job queue class (subscribers + retention + broadcast + installer + digest). Fails when depth > 10000 pending OR oldest job > 30 minutes on retention OR > 5 minutes on notifications. Stalled queues = retention doesn't purge = disk grows = eventual production incident.

probes.name: activity-schedule-registered
probes.description: Boot-time. Verifies the six scheduled entries (activity:purge daily, activity:archive-cold weekly, activity:reconcile-batches daily, digest:activity daily + weekly, activity:count daily) are registered on the scheduler. Missing entries = activity data never purges = permanent unintended retention.

probes.name: activity-orphan-batch-count
probes.description: Emits count of activity batches marked as orphaned in the last 24h. Fed by the daily ReconcileActivityBatchesJob. Non-critical but alerts fire above threshold (default 100 orphans / day — anything higher signals request crashes elsewhere on the platform).

probes.name: activity-write-latency-p95
probes.description: Runs a synthetic LogsActivity write with a fixture Booking + timer. Fails when p95 latency > 100ms over the last 60s. Non-critical (informational) — activity writes are the tail on domain writes; latency here directly impacts user-perceived request time.
